1→# Gossamer
     2→
     3→**Never lose the context behind your code again.**
     4→
     5→Gossamer is a desktop control panel for your Claude Code sessions. It indexes, searches, and backs up every AI session you've ever run — so the thinking, the decisions, the rabbit holes, the breakthroughs — all of it is preserved and searchable forever.
     6→
     7→And because Gossamer integrates with **[Entire.io](https://entire.io)**, your AI sessions are linked directly to your Git commits. Every line of code has a story. Now you can actually read it.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## What it does
    12→
    13→- **Index** every Claude Code session automatically as you work
    14→- **Search** across all your sessions — find that conversation where you figured out the auth bug three months ago
    15→- **Back up** sessions so nothing is ever lost, even across machines
    16→- **Link sessions to commits** via Entire.io — click any commit and see the full AI conversation behind it
    17→- **Browse checkpoints** — step through the evolution of a session with diffs and context at every turn
    18→- **Resume sessions** — jump back into past conversations right where you left off
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Requirements
    23→
    24→- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v18+
    25→- [Entire CLI](https://entire.io) installed
    26→- Claude Code installed and configured
    27→
    28→> **Homebrew installs coming soon.** For now, clone and run locally.
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## Getting started
    33→
    34→### 1. Install the Entire CLI and enable it in your repo
    35→
    36→Install the [Entire CLI](https://entire.io), then run the following inside any repo you want Gossamer to track:
    37→
    38→```bash
    39→entire enable
    40→```
    41→
    42→This hooks into your Git workflow so Gossamer can link AI sessions to commits.
    43→
    44→### 2. Clone Gossamer and start it
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
    48→cd gossamer
    49→npm run start
    50→```
    51→
    52→`npm run start` installs dependencies, builds, and launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together.
    53→
    54→### 3. Add your repo in the Repos tab
    55→
    56→Open Gossamer, go to the **Repos** tab, and add the local path to the repo you ran `entire enable` in. Gossamer will start indexing your sessions immediately.
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## Architecture
    61→
    62→Gossamer has two components that run together:
    63→
    64→| Component | What it does |
    65→|-----------|-------------|
    66→| **Server** (`src/serve.ts`) | Node.js + Express + WebSocket server. Indexes Claude Code sessions from disk, syncs with Entire.io, and serves a real-time API on `localhost:3000`. |
    67→| **App** (`app/`) | Electron desktop app built with React + Mantine. Connects to the local server and renders your session history, search, checkpoints, and diffs. |
    68→
    69→Both start with `npm start`.
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## How the Entire.io integration works
    74→
    75→Gossamer uses the Entire CLI to associate your Claude Code sessions with the Git commits they produced. When you make a commit while Claude Code is active, Entire captures the link between the session and the commit hash.
    76→
    77→From Gossamer you can:
    78→- See which session produced which commit
    79→- Open a commit and read the full AI conversation that led to it
    80→- Never wonder "why did we write it this way?" again
    81→
    82→Make sure the Entire CLI is installed and you're logged in before starting Gossamer.
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→## Your data, your machine
    87→
    88→Gossamer is built on a simple principle: your AI sessions are yours. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no third-party storage. Everything stays local by default.
    89→
    90→- **Search index and database** are stored in a single SQLite file per repo at `~/.gossamer/<YOUR_REPO>.db` — on your machine, readable with any SQLite tool, deletable whenever you want.
    91→- **Session history** is stored in your local Git repository. Push it to your own remote to back it up or share it across machines — on your terms, using infrastructure you already control.
    92→- **The server is local.** Gossamer's backend runs on `localhost` and never opens an external connection. The Electron app talks only to that local server.
    93→
    94→You decide what gets backed up, who has access, and when it gets deleted. Gossamer never touches your data without you.
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Development
    99→
   100→To work on the server and app separately:
   101→
   102→```bash
   103→# Server only (hot-reloads with tsc --watch)
   104→npm run serve
   105→
   106→# App only (Electron + Vite HMR)
   107→cd app && npm run dev
   108→```
   109→
   110→Database UI:
   111→
   112→```bash
   113→npm run db:studio
   114→```
   115→
   116→---
   117→
   118→## Coming soon
   119→
   120→- `brew install gossamer` — one-line install, no Node required
   121→- Team sharing — share session history with your whole org
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.
   126→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me create a summary report.

---

## Project Exploration Report: Gossamer

### 1. **Package.json Summary**

**Main Package** (`/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/package.json`):
- **Name**: `claude-hooks-listener`
- **Version**: `1.0.0`
- **Type**: ES Module (`"type": "module"`)
- **Description**: "Gossamer: indexes Entire CLI logs and serves them via HTTP + WebSocket"
- **Bin Entries**: 
  - `gossamer-serve` → `./dist/serve.js`

**Scripts**:
- `build`: `prisma generate && tsc`
- `serve`: `node dist/serve.js`
- `start`: Runs server and app concurrently
- `db:push`: Initialize database
- `db:studio`: Prisma Studio UI

**Key Dependencies**:
- Prisma 7 ecosystem (`@prisma/client`, `@prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3`)
- SQLite support (`better-sqlite3`)
- Server: Express, WebSocket (ws), CORS
- Terminal: xterm, node-pty
- Git integration: gitlog
- Node v18+ required

**App Package** (`app/package.json`):
- **Name**: `gossamer-app`
- **Version**: `1.0.0`
- **Type**: Electron app (Main: `out/main/index.js`)
- **Framework**: React 18 + Mantine UI
- **Build Tool**: Electron-Vite
- **Router**: react-router-dom
- **Utilities**: diff2html (for diffs), xterm (terminal viewing)

---

### 2. **Project Structure**

**Top-level directories**:
```
/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/
├── src/               # TypeScript source (backend)
├── app/               # Electron app (frontend)
├── dist/              # Compiled backend (JavaScript)
├── prisma/            # Database schema & config
├── node_modules/      # Dependencies
├── .git/              # Git repo
├── .github/           # Not present (no CI/CD found)
├── .claude/           # Claude Code config
├── .gossamer/         # Gossamer local config
├── package.json       # Root package config
├── tsconfig.json      # TypeScript config
├── prisma.config.ts   # Prisma 7 config
└── README.md          # Project documentation
```

---

### 3. **Built Output (dist/ Directory)**

Contains compiled JavaScript from src/:
- `config.js` / `config.d.ts` — Configuration management
- `db.js` / `db.d.ts` — Database helpers (Prisma integration)
- `gitUtils.js` / `gitUtils.d.ts` — Git utilities
- `indexer.js` / `indexer.d.ts` — Session indexing logic (35KB)
- `search.js` / `search.d.ts` — Search functionality
- `serve.js` / `serve.d.ts` — Entry point for `gossamer-serve` bin
- `server.js` / `server.d.ts` — Express + WebSocket server (56KB)
- `index.js` / `index.d.ts` — Legacy/alternate entry (10KB)
- `handler.js` / `handler.d.ts` — Hook handler (51KB)

All `.js` files in dist/ are executable (marked with `rwxr-xr-x`) and dated Mar 13, 2025.

---

### 4. **TypeScript Configuration**

**tsconfig.json**:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "declaration": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

---

### 5. **CI/CD Configuration**

**Status**: None found
- No `.github/workflows/` directory
- No `.yml` / `.yaml` files at top level
- Project relies on local `npm` commands for build

---

### 6. **README.md Summary**

**Project**: Gossamer — "Never lose the context behind your code again."

**Core Features**:
- Index Claude Code sessions automatically
- Search across all sessions (find conversations from months ago)
- Backup sessions locally
- Link sessions to Git commits via Entire.io
- Browse session checkpoints with diffs
- Resume past sessions

**Architecture**:
- **Server** (`src/serve.ts`): Node.js + Express + WebSocket on `localhost:3000`
- **App** (`app/`): Electron desktop app (React + Mantine)

**Data Storage**:
- SQLite database per repo at `~/.gossamer/<REPO>.db`
- 100% local — no cloud, no telemetry
- Uses Git as backup transport (push to your own remote)

**Dev Environment**:
- Requires Node.js v18+, Entire CLI, Claude Code
- `npm run start` launches server + app together
- `npm run db:studio` for database UI

---

### 7. **Source Code Structure** (`src/`)

- `config.ts` — Configuration management
- `db.ts` — SQLite/Prisma database helpers
- `gitUtils.ts` — Git integration (with gitlog)
- `indexer.ts` — Session indexing and event processing (main logic)
- `search.ts` — Search implementation
- `serve.ts` — Entry point (spawns server.ts)
- `server.ts` — Express HTTP + WebSocket API server

---

## Key Insights

1. **Two-part architecture**: Backend (Node.js server) + Frontend (Electron desktop app)
2. **Database**: SQLite with Prisma 7 using better-sqlite3 adapter
3. **Real-time sync**: WebSocket broadcasts between local server and Electron app
4. **No external dependencies**: All data stays local, no cloud integrations
5. **Built-in terminal**: xterm + node-pty for embedded terminal sessions
6. **Active development**: Last commits March 20-22, 2025 (tabbed interface, subsessions)
7. **No release pipeline**: Manual builds only; suitable for direct git clone + npm install

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me create a summary report.

---

## Project Exploration Report: Gossamer

### 1. **Package.json Summary**

**Main Package** (`/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/package.json`):
- **Name**: `claude-hooks-listener`
- **Version**: `1.0.0`
- **Type**: ES Module (`"type": "module"`)
- **Description**: "Gossamer: indexes Entire CLI logs and serves them via HTTP + WebSocket"
- **Bin Entries**: 
  - `gossamer-serve` → `./dist/serve.js`

**Scripts**:
- `build`: `prisma generate && tsc`
- `serve`: `node dist/serve.js`
- `start`: Runs server and app concurrently
- `db:push`: Initialize database
- `db:studio`: Prisma Studio UI

**Key Dependencies**:
- Prisma 7 ecosystem (`@prisma/client`, `@prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3`)
- SQLite support (`better-sqlite3`)
- Server: Express, WebSocket (ws), CORS
- Terminal: xterm, node-pty
- Git integration: gitlog
- Node v18+ required

**App Package** (`app/package.json`):
- **Name**: `gossamer-app`
- **Version**: `1.0.0`
- **Type**: Electron app (Main: `out/main/index.js`)
- **Framework**: React 18 + Mantine UI
- **Build Tool**: Electron-Vite
- **Router**: react-router-dom
- **Utilities**: diff2html (for diffs), xterm (terminal viewing)

---

### 2. **Project Structure**

**Top-level directories**:
```
/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/
├── src/               # TypeScript source (backend)
├── app/               # Electron app (frontend)
├── dist/              # Compiled backend (JavaScript)
├── prisma/            # Database schema & config
├── node_modules/      # Dependencies
├── .git/              # Git repo
├── .github/           # Not present (no CI/CD found)
├── .claude/           # Claude Code config
├── .gossamer/         # Gossamer local config
├── package.json       # Root package config
├── tsconfig.json      # TypeScript config
├── prisma.config.ts   # Prisma 7 config
└── README.md          # Project documentation
```

---

### 3. **Built Output (dist/ Directory)**

Contains compiled JavaScript from src/:
- `config.js` / `config.d.ts` — Configuration management
- `db.js` / `db.d.ts` — Database helpers (Prisma integration)
- `gitUtils.js` / `gitUtils.d.ts` — Git utilities
- `indexer.js` / `indexer.d.ts` — Session indexing logic (35KB)
- `search.js` / `search.d.ts` — Search functionality
- `serve.js` / `serve.d.ts` — Entry point for `gossamer-serve` bin
- `server.js` / `server.d.ts` — Express + WebSocket server (56KB)
- `index.js` / `index.d.ts` — Legacy/alternate entry (10KB)
- `handler.js` / `handler.d.ts` — Hook handler (51KB)

All `.js` files in dist/ are executable (marked with `rwxr-xr-x`) and dated Mar 13, 2025.

---

### 4. **TypeScript Configuration**

**tsconfig.json**:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "declaration": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

---

### 5. **CI/CD Configuration**

**Status**: None found
- No `.github/workflows/` directory
- No `.yml` / `.yaml` files at top level
- Project relies on local `npm` commands for build

---

### 6. **README.md Summary**

**Project**: Gossamer — "Never lose the context behind your code again."

**Core Features**:
- Index Claude Code sessions automatically
- Search across all sessions (find conversations from months ago)
- Backup sessions locally
- Link sessions to Git commits via Entire.io
- Browse session checkpoints with diffs
- Resume past sessions

**Architecture**:
- **Server** (`src/serve.ts`): Node.js + Express + WebSocket on `localhost:3000`
- **App** (`app/`): Electron desktop app (React + Mantine)

**Data Storage**:
- SQLite database per repo at `~/.gossamer/<REPO>.db`
- 100% local — no cloud, no telemetry
- Uses Git as backup transport (push to your own remote)

**Dev Environment**:
- Requires Node.js v18+, Entire CLI, Claude Code
- `npm run start` launches server + app together
- `npm run db:studio` for database UI

---

### 7. **Source Code Structure** (`src/`)

- `config.ts` — Configuration management
- `db.ts` — SQLite/Prisma database helpers
- `gitUtils.ts` — Git integration (with gitlog)
- `indexer.ts` — Session indexing and event processing (main logic)
- `search.ts` — Search implementation
- `serve.ts` — Entry point (spawns server.ts)
- `server.ts` — Express HTTP + WebSocket API server

---

## Key Insights

1. **Two-part architecture**: Backend (Node.js server) + Frontend (Electron desktop app)
2. **Database**: SQLite with Prisma 7 using better-sqlite3 adapter
3. **Real-time sync**: WebSocket broadcasts between local server and Electron app
4. **No external dependencies**: All data stays local, no cloud integrations
5. **Built-in terminal**: xterm + node-pty for embedded terminal sessions
6. **Active development**: Last commits March 20-22, 2025 (tabbed interface, subsessions)
7. **No release pipeline**: Manual builds only; suitable for direct git clone + npm install

{
  "name": "claude-hooks-listener",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Gossamer: indexes Entire CLI logs and serves them via HTTP + WebSocket",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": {
    "gossamer-serve": "./dist/serve.js"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "postinstall": "node -e \"if(process.platform!=='win32'){require('fs').chmodSync('node_modules/node-pty/prebuilds/'+process.platform+'-'+process.arch+'/spawn-helper',0o755)}\" 2>/dev/null || true",
    "build": "prisma generate && tsc",
    "serve": "node dist/serve.js",
    "start": "concurrently --names \"serve,app\" -c \"blue,green\" \"npm run serve\" \"cd app && npm run dev\"",
    "db:push": "DATABASE_URL=\"file:$HOME/.claude/hook-handler.db\" prisma db push",
    "db:studio": "prisma studio"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3": "^7.4.2",
    "@prisma/client": "^7.4.2",
    "@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.11.0",
    "@xterm/xterm": "^6.0.0",
    "better-sqlite3": "^12.6.2",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "express": "^4.18.2",
    "gitlog": "^5.1.0",
    "node-pty": "^1.1.0",
    "ws": "^8.18.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.13",
    "@types/cors": "^2.8.17",
    "@types/express": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/ws": "^8.5.13",
    "concurrently": "^9.2.1",
    "prisma": "^7.4.2",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0"
  }
}

content.text:
Good — the Electron app is a pure UI that connects to the `gossamer-serve` backend. So packaging involves two parts:
1. **Cask** for the Electron `.app`
2. **Formula** (or npm) for the `gossamer-serve` CLI

Here's what I'll create:
- `app/package.json` — add `electron-builder` + build config
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — build macOS DMG on tag push, publish to GitHub Releases
- `homebrew/gossamer.rb` — Cask template (goes in your tap repo)

content.text: Now create the GitHub Actions release workflow:

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

input.content:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build-macos:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install app dependencies
        working-directory: app
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build and package Electron app (macOS)
        working-directory: app
        env:
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: false
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: npm run dist:mac

      - name: Upload to GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: |
            app/release/**/*.dmg
            app/release/**/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Install app dependencies

      - name: Build and package Electron app (macOS)

          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: npm run dist:mac

        with:
          files: |
            app/release/**/*.dmg
            app/release/**/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

content.text: Now create the Cask formula template:

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

input.content:
cask "gossamer" do
  version "1.0.0"

  on_arm do
    sha256 "REPLACE_WITH_ARM64_SHA256"
    url "https://github.com/nosman/gossamer/releases/download/v#{version}/Gossamer-#{version}-arm64.dmg"
  end

  on_intel do
    sha256 "REPLACE_WITH_X64_SHA256"
    url "https://github.com/nosman/gossamer/releases/download/v#{version}/Gossamer-#{version}-x64.dmg"
  end

  name "Gossamer"
  desc "Browse and search your Claude Code sessions locally"
  homepage "https://github.com/nosman/gossamer"

  app "Gossamer.app"

  postflight do
    puts <<~EOS
      Gossamer requires the gossamer-serve backend to be running.
      Install it with:
        npm install -g claude-hooks-listener
      Then run:
        gossamer-serve
    EOS
  end
end

content.text:
---

Here's the full process to make it available to others:

## What was created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `app/package.json` | Added `electron-builder` + `build` config + `dist:mac` script |
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Builds the `.dmg` and publishes to GitHub Releases on any `v*` tag |
| `homebrew/gossamer.rb` | Cask template to copy into your tap repo |

## To publish a release

```bash
# 1. Install electron-builder
cd app && npm install

# 2. Bump the version in app/package.json if needed, then tag and push
git tag v1.0.0
git push origin v1.0.0
```

GitHub Actions will build `Gossamer-1.0.0-arm64.dmg` and `Gossamer-1.0.0-x64.dmg` and attach them to the release automatically.

## To make it `brew install`-able

You need a **tap repo** — a GitHub repo named `homebrew-gossamer` under your account.

```bash
# Create repo at github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer
# Then copy the cask file into it:

mkdir -p Casks
cp gossamer/homebrew/gossamer.rb Casks/gossamer.rb
```

After the release runs, get the SHA256 of each DMG:
```bash
shasum -a 256 Gossamer-1.0.0-arm64.dmg
shasum -a 256 Gossamer-1.0.0-x64.dmg
```

Update the `REPLACE_WITH_*` placeholders in `Casks/gossamer.rb` and push. Then users can install with:

```bash
brew tap nosman/gossamer
brew install --cask gossamer
```

## One caveat

The app is **unsigned** (no Apple Developer cert), so on first launch macOS will block it. Users need to: **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway**, or you can right-click the `.app` → Open.

To remove this friction long-term you'd need an Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and add signing secrets to the GitHub Actions workflow.
